\paragraph{Snapshot Results.}

\paragraph{Scale Effects.}

\paragraph{Reasoning Contrast.}
At the largest currently available size-matched contrast, \texttt{\DayOneScaleReasoningLargestMatchedInstructModel} versus \texttt{\DayOneScaleReasoningLargestMatchedReasoningModel} at \DayOneScaleReasoningLargestMatchedSize{}, the reasoning model changes action accuracy by \DayOneScaleReasoningLargestMatchedDeltaActionAcc{}, utility by \DayOneScaleReasoningLargestMatchedDeltaUtility{}, over-answer rate by \DayOneScaleReasoningLargestMatchedDeltaOverAnswer{}, and JSON parse rate by \DayOneScaleReasoningLargestMatchedDeltaJsonParse{} relative to the instruct baseline.

\paragraph{Intro Preview.}
Across completed open baselines, scale clearly improves action calibration, but \DayOneScaleReasoningHardestSlice{} remains the key bottleneck and the final instruct-versus-reasoning claim should wait for the missing size-matched checkpoints.
